title: Download all your notes from Settings
summary: Adds a button in Settings that downloads every note you have as a single zip file, so you can keep your own backup. It also adds a shared, signed-in way for the app to fetch file downloads from the server.
motivation: Expose the backend streaming ZIP note export from settings and add a reusable authenticated raw-response path.

included: GET /api/settings/export-notes remains the backend streaming ZIP contract
export filename, metadata, and ZIP generation move from router code into settings service helpers
the frontend can request an authenticated Blob/Response through the existing HTTP/auth hook policy
SettingsView gains an export action that downloads the backend ZIP
excluded: single-note export changes in the note overflow menu
custom export filters, date ranges, or alternate formats
new download dependencies or File System Access API usage

design_facts.stack: Mixed Vue 3/TypeScript/Vite frontend plus FastAPI/Python 3.13/SQLAlchemy async/PostgreSQL backend; DTCG design tokens; no router or Pinia.

token_manifest.system: W3C DTCG JSON built with Style Dictionary into CSS custom properties.
token_manifest.consumption_rule: New component CSS consumes semantic custom properties only; primitives are not used in new code.

work_items.title: Bundle your notes into one download
work_items.summary: Moves the work of gathering a person's notes and packaging them into a downloadable zip out of the request handler and into a shared service, while keeping each person's notes private to them.

functions: async collect_notes_for_export(db: AsyncSession, user_id: int) -> tuple[list[tuple[str, str]], list[dict[str, Any]]]

behavior: all Note reads remain tenant-scoped with Note.user_id == user_id
duplicate and unsafe note titles still produce unique safe .md filenames
metadata JSON still includes id, title, content, createdAt, and updatedAt for every exported note
the route streams application/zip with Content-Disposition containing parchmark_notes_<timestamp>.zip
routers/settings.py delegates export collection/response assembly to the settings service module

signals: sensitive-zone:bulk export of all user notes
blast-radius:router export helpers move into services

oracle.command: make test-backend-lint && make test-backend-format && make test-backend-types && cd backend && uv run pytest tests/integration/test_settings.py::TestExportNotes -v
assertions: Export still streams a valid ZIP for empty, normal, duplicate-title, and large note sets.
The metadata JSON shape and Content-Disposition filename remain unchanged.
The export query remains scoped to the current user's notes.

work_items.title: Save all your notes to your device
work_items.summary: Adds an export button to the Settings screen that asks the server for the notes zip and saves it to the reader's device, showing a loading state while it works and a clear message if something goes wrong.

component_map.notes: Native button with loading and error text; use Blob URL download only after backend response succeeds.

exposes.http: an authenticated raw/blob response helper in ui/src/services/http.ts

behavior: the HTTP helper attaches the bearer token and preserves the existing single refresh-and-retry policy for 401s
exportNotes calls GET /settings/export-notes and returns a Blob plus filename parsed from Content-Disposition, falling back to parchmark_notes.zip
SettingsView disables the export button while pending, shows errors inline, and creates/revokes exactly one object URL per successful download
the existing note overflow single-note export remains local and unchanged

oracle.command: make test-ui-lint && cd ui && npm run test -- src/services/http.test.ts src/services/settings.test.ts src/features/settings/useSettings.test.ts src/features/settings/SettingsView.test.ts
assertions: The raw/blob HTTP helper sends Authorization, refreshes once on 401, and surfaces ApiError details on failure.
exportNotes parses the backend filename from Content-Disposition and falls back when absent.
SettingsView downloads the Blob through URL.createObjectURL, clicks a temporary anchor, revokes the URL, and handles pending/error states.
